Media sent video received from the public which showed a well defined landspout, with time described as early afternoon. LSR time approximated by satellite data accordingly. Landspout location also approximated by the video.
Time and location estimated from photo sent to broadcast media and corroborated with radar.
Mesonet station UTBEV I-15 at Beaver.
Mesonet station VRH Vernon Hill.
Mesonet station UCC01 Snowville (West).
Mesonet station UTSTV US-40 at Starvation.
USGS Escalante River gauge (ESCU1) spiked to 2491 cfs as the flood wave moved through.
Media reported that 4 hikers were rescued on the Bighorn Canyon Trail after being stranded due to flash flooding along Harris Wash. Video also showed flash flooding of the normally dry wash.
Minor flash flooding observed in Spring Canyon at the confluence with the Fremont River. Time estimated from radar.
Picture on social media of broken 1 inch diameter tree branches in Bloomington.
Broken 1 inch diameter tree branches reported via mping.

Snow Canyon State Park employee reported damage to West Canyon Road due to flooding. A tent was also flooded forcing a camper to sleep in their vehicle. Rainfall estimates in this vicinity indicated between .70 and .90 inches of rain had fallen in around 90 minutes. Time estimated by radar.
Park rangers reported a flash flood in Sulphur Creek at Capitol Reef National Park.
Penny to quarter hail in Sunnyside.
Visitor to the Grand Staircase Escalante visitor center reported flash flooding in Big Horn Canyon.
ASOS station KMLF Milford Municipal Airport.
Thunderstorm wind gusts damaged the main camp. Three staked port a potties were blown over. Shower unit canopy was blown over, several tents rolled away including one that flew about 100 yards.

Pictures from social media showed a mature pine tree uprooted as well as a damaged gazebo. Time and location estimated by radar and the nearest mesonet observation in Clinton which recorded a gust to 43 mph. Thunderstorm outflow that caused the damage had a history of producing gusts of 50 to 60 mph.
Large spruce with root ball knocked over in microburst. Crushed two cars in a driveway.

Heavy rainfall from a thunderstorm falling over steep terrain Sunday evening in Nephi Canyon caused flooding and an associated closure of SR 132 at mile marker 35. A railroad weather station located near the top of the adjacent canyon recorded 0.59 inches of rain in 10 minutes and 0.90 inches of rain 30 minutes associated with the thunderstorm. Location and time are estimated.
Mesonet recorded wind gust of 59 mph at the Horizontal Grid sensor (DPG08).
Mesonet recorded wind gust of 63 mph at the Playa Station sensor (DPG09).
Mesonet recorded wind gust of 65 mph at the Target S sensor (DPG04).
Mesonet recorded wind gust of 62 mph at the Simpson Springs sensor (DPG21).
Flash flooding ongoing in the Main Canyon with muddy debris across the road. The East Fork of the Virgin River rose approximately 1.5 feet.
Flash flooding ongoing in the main canyon including Clear Creek.
Estimated time based on radar and gauge data from Lees Ferry.
Flash flooding reported along Coyote Wash near US-89 milepost 9.
Buckhorn Road washed out, car on road washed a quarter mile down the road. Four people were rescued due to flash flooding.
Flash flood reported along Wahweap Creek near Big Water.
San Rafael River Gauge reports flood wave around 700 cfs.
Flood wave reported on the East Fork of the Virgin River at 510 cfs.
Significant flash flood reported at the North Fork of the Virgin River gauge rising to 1290 cfs.
Debris flow out of recent Buckley Draw burn scar that closed down portions of Nevada Avenue and Slate Canyon Drive.
~300 cfs flood wave measured at US-89 gauge.

*** 1 INJ *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 20 WSW Bullfrog. Flash flood in Hurricane Wash carried a hiker 300 feet downstream before climbing a tree to escape the flood. Hiker needed helicopter evacuation for medical care. Washouts also reported on Hole-in-the-Rock Road with seven vehicles stranded. Time estimated based on RADAR.

*** 1 INJ *** Flash flood in Hurricane Wash carried a hiker 300 feet downstream before climbing a tree to escape the flood. Hiker needed helicopter evacuation for medical care. Washouts also reported on Hole-in-the-Rock Road. Time estimated based on RADAR.
Flood wave around 700 cfs hit the Dirty Devil Gauge.

Multiple groups were trapped and had to seek high ground during a flash flood in Little Wildhorse Canyon. Search and rescue was called and all parties were eventually able to hike out safely.
Flash flood along Paria River approximately 2 miles below White House campground, requiring helicopter evacuations of stranded hikers.
The Dirty Devil River at Poison Springs above Hanksville crested at 604 cfs.
Mesonet station UR305 27.0 E Lucin.
Reports of numerous washouts in dry washes and slot canyons on the east side of Zion NP following heavy rainfall.
Report of mud and sand covering SR-9 at the Washington-Kane County line after heavy rainfall. Heavy equipment required to clear the road.

USGS River Gauge on the North Fork of the Virgin River reported a sharp rise from a baseline of 45 cfs to 869 cfs within 15 minutes. a rise of 3 feet. In addition Zion National Park reported high flows on Clear Creek.
Mesonet station UTDVC I-15 At Mp 139 Dog Valley Creek.
Mesonet station UTRCH I-70 at Richfield.

Washington County Emergency Management and local Law Enforcement reported multiple debris flows and flash flooding across several roads in and around pine valley with road closures in effect. One debris flow was reported 150 feet wide and 6 to 8 inches deep. Time obtained from peak flows on Santa Clara River gauge at Pine Valley.

Reports from Zion NP dispatch of mud & rock covering Kolob Canyon Road. Debris required heavy equipment to clear. Report was received in the morning, but likely occurred from heavy rainfall during the overnight hours.
Flash flood reported on Buckskin Gulch. Time estimated.
2400cfs flood wave hit the Paria River gauge at US-89.
Flood wave on the East Fork of the Virgin River hit the gauge at 1220 cfs.
Flood wave hit the gauge on the North Fork of the Virgin River at 1235 AM, peak flow was 450 cfs.
1210 cfs flood wave on Fort Pearce Wash hit the gauge at 5 AM.
Trained spotter reported Wahweap was flashing. Time is approximate.

Mesonet station PC490 Kennecott Rd Lake Point.

Backcountry guide reported flash flooding of Catstair Canyon along HWY 89 in addition to all small washes between mile post 19 and 21 along HWY 89. Some amount of water was still flowing down the Paria at 3 pm.
